1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# Launch a TensorRT-LLM OpenAI-compatible API server from a model config YAML.
3	#
4	# Usage (via dispatcher — preferred):
5	#   ./serving/launch.sh <model_config_trtllm.yaml> [--kv-cache-dtype <dtype>]
6	#                       [--port <port>] [--max-model-len <n>]
7	#
8	# Direct usage:
9	#   ./serving/launch_trtllm.sh <model_config_trtllm.yaml> [OPTIONS]
10	#
11	# Reads from YAML config (serving.*):
12	#   model.name               → positional model arg (when source: "hf")
13	#   serving.source           → "hf" (default) or "engine_dir"
14	#   serving.engine_dir       → path to pre-built TRT engine (source: "engine_dir")
15	#   serving.max_batch_size   (default 256) → --max_batch_size
16	#   serving.max_num_tokens   (default 8192) → --max_num_tokens
17	#   serving.tp_size          (default 1) → --tp_size
18	#   serving.port             (default 8000) → --port (overridable via CLI)
19	#
20	# KV-cache dtype:
21	#   TRT-LLM KV-cache quantization (fp8) must be set at engine-build time
22	#   via `trtllm-build --kv_cache_type fp8`. Runtime overrides via
23	#   --kv-cache-dtype are logged as a warning and ignored in JIT-from-HF mode.
24	#   Only "auto" is accepted silently at runtime.
25	#
26	# Two engine modes:
27	#   source: "hf"          — JIT compile on first launch (5-15 min for 30B).
28	#                           Recommended for first-run smoke tests.
29	#   source: "engine_dir"  — Fast launch from a pre-built engine (see
30	#                           scripts/build_trtllm_engines.sh for the build step).
31	
32	set -euo pipefail
33	
34	LAUNCH_SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
35	LAUNCH_PROJECT_ROOT="$(cd "$LAUNCH_SCRIPT_DIR/.." && pwd)"
36	
37	# Activate the TRT-LLM venv if no venv is currently active.
38	if [ -z "${VIRTUAL_ENV:-}" ] && [ -f "$LAUNCH_PROJECT_ROOT/.venv-trtllm/bin/activate" ]; then
39	    # shellcheck disable=SC1091
40	    source "$LAUNCH_PROJECT_ROOT/.venv-trtllm/bin/activate"
41	fi
42	
43	if [ $# -lt 1 ]; then
44	    echo "Usage: $0 <model_config_trtllm.yaml> [--kv-cache-dtype <dtype>] [--port <port>]"
45	    exit 1
46	fi
47	
48	CONFIG_FILE="$1"
49	shift
50	
51	if [ ! -f "$CONFIG_FILE" ]; then
52	    echo "Error: Config file not found: $CONFIG_FILE" >&2
53	    exit 1
54	fi
55	
56	# shellcheck source=serving/_yaml_helper.sh
57	source "$LAUNCH_SCRIPT_DIR/_yaml_helper.sh"
58	
59	MODEL_NAME=$(parse_yaml "model.name" "")
60	SOURCE=$(parse_yaml "serving.source" "hf")
61	ENGINE_DIR=$(parse_yaml "serving.engine_dir" "")
62	MAX_BATCH=$(parse_yaml "serving.max_batch_size" "256")
63	MAX_TOKENS=$(parse_yaml "serving.max_num_tokens" "8192")
64	TP_SIZE=$(parse_yaml "serving.tp_size" "1")
65	PORT=$(parse_yaml "serving.port" "8000")
66	KV_CACHE_DTYPE="auto"
67	MAX_NUM_TOKENS_OVERRIDE=""
68	
69	while [ $# -gt 0 ]; do
70	    case "$1" in
71	        --kv-cache-dtype) KV_CACHE_DTYPE="$2";          shift 2 ;;
72	        --port)           PORT="$2";                    shift 2 ;;
73	        --max-model-len)  MAX_NUM_TOKENS_OVERRIDE="$2"; shift 2 ;;
74	        --max-num-seqs)   MAX_BATCH="$2";               shift 2 ;;
75	        *)
76	            echo "Unknown argument: $1" >&2
77	            exit 1
78	            ;;
79	    esac
80	done
81	
82	if [ -n "$MAX_NUM_TOKENS_OVERRIDE" ]; then
83	    MAX_TOKENS="$MAX_NUM_TOKENS_OVERRIDE"
84	fi
85	
86	# KV-cache dtype handling: TRT-LLM is build-time only.
87	case "$KV_CACHE_DTYPE" in
88	    ""|auto)
89	        ;;  # default — no flag needed
90	    fp8)
91	        if [[ "$SOURCE" == "hf" ]]; then
92	            echo "WARN: --kv-cache-dtype=fp8 is ignored in JIT-from-HF mode." >&2
93	            echo "      Pre-build the engine with: trtllm-build --kv_cache_type fp8" >&2
94	            echo "      Then use source: engine_dir in your YAML config." >&2
95	        fi
96	        ;;
97	    turboquant*|rotorquant*|kivi_*|kvquant*)
98	        echo "ERROR: kv-cache-dtype '$KV_CACHE_DTYPE' is vLLM-only." >&2
99	        echo "       TRT-LLM supports: auto (runtime), fp8 (build-time via trtllm-build)." >&2
100	        exit 1
101	        ;;
102	    *)
103	        echo "WARN: Unknown TRT-LLM kv-cache-dtype '$KV_CACHE_DTYPE'. Ignoring." >&2
104	        ;;
105	esac
106	
107	TRT_ARGS=(
108	    --port          "$PORT"
109	    --max_batch_size "$MAX_BATCH"
110	    --max_num_tokens "$MAX_TOKENS"
111	    --tp_size        "$TP_SIZE"
112	)
113	
114	# Determine model source
115	if [[ "$SOURCE" == "engine_dir" ]]; then
116	    if [ -z "$ENGINE_DIR" ]; then
117	        echo "ERROR: serving.source=engine_dir but serving.engine_dir is not set in $CONFIG_FILE" >&2
118	        exit 1
119	    fi
120	    MODEL_ARG="$ENGINE_DIR"
121	else
122	    if [ -z "$MODEL_NAME" ]; then
123	        echo "Error: model.name not found in config" >&2
124	        exit 1
125	    fi
126	    MODEL_ARG="$MODEL_NAME"
127	fi
128	
129	echo "=== Launching TensorRT-LLM Server ==="
130	echo "Model:       $MODEL_ARG"
131	echo "Source:      $SOURCE"
132	echo "Max Batch:   $MAX_BATCH"
133	echo "Max Tokens:  $MAX_TOKENS"
134	echo "TP Size:     $TP_SIZE"
135	echo "Port:        $PORT"
136	if [[ "$SOURCE" == "hf" ]]; then
137	    echo "NOTE: First launch compiles TRT engine (5-15 min for large models)."
138	fi
139	echo "======================================"
140	
141	exec trtllm-serve "$MODEL_ARG" "${TRT_ARGS[@]}"
142

file.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Launch a TensorRT-LLM OpenAI-compatible API server from a model config YAML.
#
# Usage (via dispatcher — preferred):
#   ./serving/launch.sh <model_config_trtllm.yaml> [--kv-cache-dtype <dtype>]
#                       [--port <port>] [--max-model-len <n>]
#
# Direct usage:
#   ./serving/launch_trtllm.sh <model_config_trtllm.yaml> [OPTIONS]
#
# Reads from YAML config (serving.*):
#   model.name               → positional model arg (when source: "hf")
#   serving.source           → "hf" (default) or "engine_dir"
#   serving.engine_dir       → path to pre-built TRT engine (source: "engine_dir")
#   serving.max_batch_size   (default 256) → --max_batch_size
#   serving.max_num_tokens   (default 8192) → --max_num_tokens
#   serving.tp_size          (default 1) → --tp_size
#   serving.port             (default 8000) → --port (overridable via CLI)
#
# KV-cache dtype:
#   TRT-LLM KV-cache quantization (fp8) must be set at engine-build time
#   via `trtllm-build --kv_cache_type fp8`. Runtime overrides via
#   --kv-cache-dtype are logged as a warning and ignored in JIT-from-HF mode.
#   Only "auto" is accepted silently at runtime.
#
# Two engine modes:
#   source: "hf"          — JIT compile on first launch (5-15 min for 30B).
#                           Recommended for first-run smoke tests.
#   source: "engine_dir"  — Fast launch from a pre-built engine (see
#                           scripts/build_trtllm_engines.sh for the build step).

set -euo pipefail

LAUNCH_SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
LAUNCH_PROJECT_ROOT="$(cd "$LAUNCH_SCRIPT_DIR/.." && pwd)"

# Activate the TRT-LLM venv if no venv is currently active.
if [ -z "${VIRTUAL_ENV:-}" ] && [ -f "$LAUNCH_PROJECT_ROOT/.venv-trtllm/bin/activate" ]; then
    # shellcheck disable=SC1091
    source "$LAUNCH_PROJECT_ROOT/.venv-trtllm/bin/activate"
fi

if [ $# -lt 1 ]; then
    echo "Usage: $0 <model_config_trtllm.yaml> [--kv-cache-dtype <dtype>] [--port <port>]"
    exit 1
fi

CONFIG_FILE="$1"
shift

if [ ! -f "$CONFIG_FILE" ]; then
    echo "Error: Config file not found: $CONFIG_FILE" >&2
    exit 1
fi

# shellcheck source=serving/_yaml_helper.sh
source "$LAUNCH_SCRIPT_DIR/_yaml_helper.sh"

MODEL_NAME=$(parse_yaml "model.name" "")
SOURCE=$(parse_yaml "serving.source" "hf")
ENGINE_DIR=$(parse_yaml "serving.engine_dir" "")
MAX_BATCH=$(parse_yaml "serving.max_batch_size" "256")
MAX_TOKENS=$(parse_yaml "serving.max_num_tokens" "8192")
TP_SIZE=$(parse_yaml "serving.tp_size" "1")
PORT=$(parse_yaml "serving.port" "8000")
KV_CACHE_DTYPE="auto"
MAX_NUM_TOKENS_OVERRIDE=""

while [ $# -gt 0 ]; do
    case "$1" in
        --kv-cache-dtype) KV_CACHE_DTYPE="$2";          shift 2 ;;
        --port)           PORT="$2";                    shift 2 ;;
        --max-model-len)  MAX_NUM_TOKENS_OVERRIDE="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --max-num-seqs)   MAX_BATCH="$2";               shift 2 ;;
        *)
            echo "Unknown argument: $1" >&2
            exit 1
            ;;
    esac
done

if [ -n "$MAX_NUM_TOKENS_OVERRIDE" ]; then
    MAX_TOKENS="$MAX_NUM_TOKENS_OVERRIDE"
fi

# KV-cache dtype handling: TRT-LLM is build-time only.
case "$KV_CACHE_DTYPE" in
    ""|auto)
        ;;  # default — no flag needed
    fp8)
        if [[ "$SOURCE" == "hf" ]]; then
            echo "WARN: --kv-cache-dtype=fp8 is ignored in JIT-from-HF mode." >&2
            echo "      Pre-build the engine with: trtllm-build --kv_cache_type fp8" >&2
            echo "      Then use source: engine_dir in your YAML config." >&2
        fi
        ;;
    turboquant*|rotorquant*|kivi_*|kvquant*)
        echo "ERROR: kv-cache-dtype '$KV_CACHE_DTYPE' is vLLM-only." >&2
        echo "       TRT-LLM supports: auto (runtime), fp8 (build-time via trtllm-build)." >&2
        exit 1
        ;;
    *)
        echo "WARN: Unknown TRT-LLM kv-cache-dtype '$KV_CACHE_DTYPE'. Ignoring." >&2
        ;;
esac

TRT_ARGS=(
    --port          "$PORT"
    --max_batch_size "$MAX_BATCH"
    --max_num_tokens "$MAX_TOKENS"
    --tp_size        "$TP_SIZE"
)

# Determine model source
if [[ "$SOURCE" == "engine_dir" ]]; then
    if [ -z "$ENGINE_DIR" ]; then
        echo "ERROR: serving.source=engine_dir but serving.engine_dir is not set in $CONFIG_FILE" >&2
        exit 1
    fi
    MODEL_ARG="$ENGINE_DIR"
else
    if [ -z "$MODEL_NAME" ]; then
        echo "Error: model.name not found in config" >&2
        exit 1
    fi
    MODEL_ARG="$MODEL_NAME"
fi

echo "=== Launching TensorRT-LLM Server ==="
echo "Model:       $MODEL_ARG"
echo "Source:      $SOURCE"
echo "Max Batch:   $MAX_BATCH"
echo "Max Tokens:  $MAX_TOKENS"
echo "TP Size:     $TP_SIZE"
echo "Port:        $PORT"
if [[ "$SOURCE" == "hf" ]]; then
    echo "NOTE: First launch compiles TRT engine (5-15 min for large models)."
fi
echo "======================================"

exec trtllm-serve "$MODEL_ARG" "${TRT_ARGS[@]}"

lastPrompt: Enable speculative decoding (Dflash) for vllm, SGLang and TensorRT-LLM. Make speculative decoding configurable through model config files or arguments in benchmark scripts.

aiTitle: Enable speculative decoding across LLM frameworks

content.text: All the line numbers and shapes check out. Let me write the final plan.

input.content:
# Enable Dflash Speculative Decoding (vLLM + SGLang + TensorRT-LLM)

## Context

Phase 1 benchmarking and Phase 3 quantization benchmarks measure throughput / latency / quality across three serving backends. Speculative decoding (SD) is a well-established way to cut decode latency 2–6× without changing model quality, but the project's three launchers (`serving/launch_vllm.sh`, `launch_sglang.sh`, `launch_trtllm.sh`) emit no SD flags today. The user wants **Dflash** (block-diffusion-based SD; arXiv 2602.06036; up to 2.5× over EAGLE-3), configurable per-engine via YAML and overridable from the benchmark runners so we can A/B "base vs +dflash" cleanly inside the existing `run_exp_*.sh` / `run_concurrency_benchmark.sh` workflow.

Dflash needs a **separate Dflash-trained draft model** paired with the target. The only public pair right now is `meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct` + `z-lab/LLaMA3.1-8B-Instruct-DFlash-UltraChat`; drafts for the project's Qwen3/Gemma/Mistral targets do not yet exist on HF Hub. Training drafts is **out of scope** for this change — this plan ships the *wiring* so that when a Dflash draft is available (HF Hub, NVIDIA ModelOpt PR #1211, or trained in-house) any of the three engines can be launched with one YAML edit or one CLI flag.

Engine support matrix as of 2026-05:
| Engine | Mode | CLI shape | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| **vLLM** | runtime | `--speculative-config '{"method":"dflash","model":"...","num_speculative_tokens":N,...}'` | "Under active development" via `speculators` subproject |
| **SGLang** | runtime | `--speculative-algorithm DFLASH --speculative-draft-model-path <path> [--speculative-num-draft-tokens N] [--speculative-dflash-block-size N] [--speculative-dflash-draft-window-size W]` | Documented; hard constraints (no dp-attn, `pp_size==1`) |
| **TRT-LLM** | **build-time** | Engine pre-built via `modelopt_recipes/general/speculative_decoding/dflash.yaml`; runtime passthrough `--speculative_decoding_mode dflash` if `trtllm-serve` exposes it | NVIDIA ModelOpt PR #1211, not yet merged |

## Approach

Extend the existing per-engine sibling YAMLs (`<model>.yaml`, `<model>_sglang.yaml`, `<model>_trtllm.yaml`) with a `serving.speculative:` sub-block using **engine-native field names** (matching the existing convention that vLLM/SGLang/TRT-LLM YAML keys are not unified). Each launcher reads only the fields it understands and emits the right server CLI args. Add four unified CLI overrides to the launchers and benchmark scripts (`--speculative-method`, `--speculative-draft-model`, `--speculative-num-tokens`, `--no-speculative`) so A/B runs need no YAML edits. Pydantic schema gets a permissive `SpeculativeConfig` sub-model (informational only — bash launchers bypass it). TRT-LLM engine builder is deferred behind a stub script (Option B: require `source: engine_dir` for Dflash; error clearly otherwise).

## YAML schema — engine-native, opt-in via `enabled: true`

### vLLM (e.g. `configs/models_exp_a/qwen3_32b.yaml`)
```yaml
serving:
  engine: "vllm"
  # ... existing fields ...
  speculative:
    enabled: true
    method: "dflash"                                # vLLM speculators method name
    draft_model: "z-lab/LLaMA3.1-8B-Instruct-DFlash-UltraChat"
    num_speculative_tokens: 5
    dflash_block_size: 4                            # optional; merged into JSON
    dflash_draft_window_size: 16                    # optional; merged into JSON
    extra: {}                                       # escape hatch — forward-compat
```
Emits: `--speculative-config '{"method":"dflash","model":"...","num_speculative_tokens":5,"dflash_block_size":4,"dflash_draft_window_size":16}'`

### SGLang (e.g. `configs/models_exp_a/qwen3_32b_sglang.yaml`)
```yaml
serving:
  engine: "sglang"
  # ... existing fields ...
  speculative:
    enabled: true
    algorithm: "DFLASH"
    draft_model_path: "z-lab/LLaMA3.1-8B-Instruct-DFlash-UltraChat"
    num_draft_tokens: 5
    dflash_block_size: 4
    dflash_draft_window_size: 16
```
Emits the five `--speculative-*` flags listed in the matrix.

### TRT-LLM (e.g. `configs/models_exp_a/qwen3_32b_trtllm.yaml`)
```yaml
serving:
  engine: "tensorrt_llm"
  source: "engine_dir"                              # MANDATORY when speculative.enabled
  engine_dir: "checkpoints/trt-engines/qwen3_32b_bf16_dflash"
  # ... existing fields ...
  speculative:
    enabled: true
    mode: "dflash"                                  # → --speculative_decoding_mode dflash
    build_recipe: "modelopt_recipes/general/speculative_decoding/dflash.yaml"
    build_params:                                   # echoed in banner; not forwarded
      block_size: 4
      num_layers: 1
      mask_token_id: 0
      num_anchors: 16
      attention_heads: 8
```

**Design call — keep `num_speculative_tokens` and `dflash_block_size` as two fields.** They control different things: `num_speculative_tokens` is the per-step proposal count (tunable at serve time); `dflash_block_size` is a draft-architecture parameter that must match the trained draft. Collapsing them loses A/B flexibility.

## Files to modify

### `serving/launch_vllm.sh` (vLLM launcher)
Parse the new keys after L74 with the existing inline-python helper (extend to handle dict→JSON for `extra`). Build the speculative JSON in a single python block parallel to the `HF_OVERRIDES` block at L80-87. Append `--speculative-config "$SPEC_JSON"` to `VLLM_ARGS` after L150.

Extend the CLI argparse while-loop (L100-111) with:
- `--speculative-method <name>`
- `--speculative-draft-model <path>`
- `--speculative-num-tokens <int>`
- `--no-speculative` (force-disable; CLI > YAML, mirroring `--max-model-len` precedence at L113-116)

Add a `WARN` (do not block) when `KV_CACHE_DTYPE` matches `turboquant*` or `rotorquant*` AND `speculative.enabled=true`: the case-arm at L170-177 forwards argv unchanged, so `--speculative-config` reaches the in-process launcher untouched — but the engine-config hook in `launch_vllm_turboquant.py` patches `is_hybrid`/`is_mm_prefix_lm` during `create_engine_config`, which is where the draft model also loads. The combo is **untested**; print a one-line warning naming the interaction.

### `serving/launch_sglang.sh` (SGLang launcher)
Parse the new keys after L74. Append the five `--speculative-*` flags to `SGLANG_ARGS` after L143. Extend the argparse while-loop (L83-94) with the same four unified overrides; translate them to SGLang's native flag names so the parent runner uses one vocabulary across engines.

**Hard guards (exit 1):** Reject `--enable-dp-attention=true` AND `speculative.enabled=true` ("DFLASH is incompatible with `--enable-dp-attention`"). Reject `pp_size > 1` (currently not parsed — add it with default 1). Warn that SGLang internally disables overlap scheduler and mixed-chunked-prefill when DFLASH is on; no flag needed from us.

### `serving/launch_trtllm.sh` (TRT-LLM launcher)
Parse `serving.speculative.enabled` and `serving.speculative.mode` after L65. Append `--speculative_decoding_mode "$SPEC_MODE"` to `TRT_ARGS` after L112 — **verify against `trtllm-serve --help`** at implementation time; if the flag is absent in our installed version, the engine config already encodes the mode and no flag is needed (no-op).

Extend the argparse while-loop (L69-80) with the same four unified overrides — but log `--speculative-draft-model` / `--speculative-num-tokens` as **ignored** with a note that these are build-time for TRT-LLM.

**Hard guards (exit 1):** Reject `speculative.enabled=true` with `source: hf` ("Dflash requires a pre-built TRT-LLM engine; see scripts/build_trtllm_engines.sh"). Reject `speculative.enabled=true` with missing `engine_dir`.

### `src/llm_workflow_agents/serving/launch_vllm_turboquant.py` and `launch_vllm_rotorquant.py`
**No argparse changes required.** Both launchers forward argv to `api_server.make_arg_parser()`, which already recognises stock vLLM's `--speculative-config`. The bash launcher resolves the unified `--speculative-method` / `--speculative-draft-model` / `--speculative-num-tokens` flags into a single `--speculative-config '<json>'` *before* invoking the python module, so these files see only stock vLLM flags.

Add a one-line docstring note to `_install_turboquant_engine_config_hook` documenting the untested interaction with speculative drafts that share Gemma-4 architecture (the `is_mm_prefix_lm` patch would propagate to the draft loader). Current Dflash drafts are Llama-3.1, so no interaction in practice.

### `src/llm_workflow_agents/config/schema.py` (Pydantic schema, L44-52)
Add a permissive `SpeculativeConfig` model with `model_config = {"extra": "allow"}` and **all fields optional**. Cover the union of vLLM/SGLang/TRT-LLM keys (`enabled`, `method`, `draft_model`, `num_speculative_tokens`, `algorithm`, `draft_model_path`, `num_draft_tokens`, `dflash_block_size`, `dflash_draft_window_size`, `mode`, `build_recipe`, `build_params`, `extra`). Add `speculative: SpeculativeConfig | None = None` to `ServingConfig`. Schema stays informational — bash launchers bypass Pydantic today.

### `scripts/run_exp_a.sh`
Extend the while-loop at L31-43 with the four unified CLI flags; forward them verbatim to the `bash "$LAUNCH_SCRIPT" "$MODEL_CONFIG" ...` invocation at L137. Add a "Speculative:" line to the banner (L84-90). **Include `_spec-<method>` (or `_spec-off`) in the result filename at L162** so A/B runs don't overwrite each other.

### `scripts/run_concurrency_benchmark.sh`
Same four CLI flags, appended to the launcher invocation at L267-270. Add `_spec-*` suffix to result filename (L219). The existing TRT-LLM 30-min `HEALTH_ITERS=360` budget (L285) covers the slower engine-load path when speculative is enabled — no change needed there.

### `scripts/build_trtllm_engines.sh` (NEW — stub)
Accept `<model_config_trtllm.yaml> --output-dir <path> [--recipe dflash]`. Echo the exact `modelopt` command that *would* run, derived from the YAML's `speculative.build_recipe` and `build_params`. Exit 1 with: "Engine builder not yet wired; NVIDIA ModelOpt PR #1211 required. Hand-build with the printed command and place the result at `serving.engine_dir`." This makes the missing-builder failure surface usefully instead of as a generic "engine_dir not found".

### `tests/test_serving_launch_dispatch.py`
Enable a `LAUNCH_PRINT_ONLY=1` env var in each launcher that replaces the final `exec` with `echo "$@"; exit 0` (one-line diff per launcher). Then add:

1. `test_vllm_launcher_emits_speculative_config_json_when_enabled` — assert printed argv contains `--speculative-config`; parse the JSON and assert the expected dict.
2. `test_vllm_launcher_omits_speculative_when_disabled` — block absent or `enabled=false` → no `--speculative-config`.
3. `test_vllm_cli_override_no_speculative_trumps_yaml` — CLI `--no-speculative` wins over YAML `enabled: true`.
4. `test_sglang_launcher_emits_dflash_flags` — verify all five `--speculative-*` flags in argv.
5. `test_sglang_rejects_dp_attention_with_speculative` — exit 1, stderr contains `dp-attention`.
6. `test_sglang_rejects_pp_size_gt_1_with_speculative` — exit 1, stderr mentions `pp_size`.
7. `test_trtllm_rejects_hf_source_with_speculative` — exit 1, stderr mentions `engine_dir`.
8. `test_trtllm_emits_speculative_mode_when_engine_dir` — `source: engine_dir` + mocked-existing dir → argv contains `--speculative_decoding_mode dflash`.
9. `test_speculative_config_pydantic_roundtrip` — load each engine YAML through the Pydantic schema and assert `serving.speculative.enabled is True`.
10. `test_run_exp_a_forwards_speculative_cli` — invoke `run_exp_a.sh --dry-run ...` and grep the dry-run output for the forwarded flags.

## Verification (end-to-end)

Until a Qwen/Gemma/Mistral Dflash draft exists, smoke-test with the public Llama-3.1 pair via temporary triplet `configs/models_exp_a/llama31_8b_dflash{,_sglang,_trtllm}.yaml`.

```bash
# vLLM
bash serving/launch.sh configs/models_exp_a/llama31_8b_dflash.yaml --port 8000
curl -s http://localhost:8000/v1/chat/completions -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"model":"meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct","messages":[{"role":"user","content":"Hello"}],"max_tokens":64}' | jq .
# Confirm `--speculative-config` in vLLM startup log; check
# `vllm:spec_decode_num_accepted_tokens_total` Prometheus counter is non-zero.

# SGLang
bash serving/launch.sh configs/models_exp_a/llama31_8b_dflash_sglang.yaml --port 8000
# Verify SGLang banner shows speculative-algorithm=DFLASH; /get_server_info reports a draft worker.

# TRT-LLM (requires pre-built engine; otherwise verify the guard fires)
bash serving/launch.sh configs/models_exp_a/llama31_8b_dflash_trtllm.yaml --port 8000

# A/B differential via the runner
bash scripts/run_concurrency_benchmark.sh --config configs/models_exp_a/llama31_8b.yaml \
     --speculative-method dflash \
     --speculative-draft-model z-lab/LLaMA3.1-8B-Instruct-DFlash-UltraChat \
     --speculative-num-tokens 5
bash scripts/run_concurrency_benchmark.sh --config configs/models_exp_a/llama31_8b.yaml --no-speculative
# Compare *_spec-dflash vs *_spec-off result JSONs: TPOT should drop; acceptance rate should be reported.

# Negative path
bash serving/launch.sh configs/models_exp_a/qwen3_32b_trtllm.yaml  # source=hf + speculative → exit 1
# Manually set serving.enable_dp_attention=true in an SGLang config → exit 1 with "dp-attention" in stderr.

# Test suite
.venv/bin/pytest tests/test_serving_launch_dispatch.py -v
```

## Out of scope (explicitly deferred)

- Training Dflash draft models for Qwen3 / Gemma / Mistral targets (depends on ModelOpt PR #1211 or SafeAILab tooling).
- TRT-LLM engine build automation (`scripts/build_trtllm_engines.sh` is a stub; real builder lands once PR #1211 is merged and we have a draft to build with).
- Updating `eval/agent_benchmark.py` payload shapes — Dflash is transparent to the OpenAI-compatible API; no client-side change needed.
- Per-engine spec-decode metric scraping (acceptance rate, draft TPOT) — could go in `eval/concurrency_benchmark.py` as a follow-up; this plan only ensures the engines run with SD enabled.

## Critical files (modified)
- `serving/launch_vllm.sh`
- `serving/launch_sglang.sh`
- `serving/launch_trtllm.sh`
- `src/llm_workflow_agents/serving/launch_vllm_turboquant.py` (docstring only)
- `src/llm_workflow_agents/config/schema.py`
- `scripts/run_exp_a.sh`
- `scripts/run_concurrency_benchmark.sh`
- `tests/test_serving_launch_dispatch.py`
- Per-model YAMLs in `configs/models_exp_a/` and `configs/models_exp_bc/` — opt-in `serving.speculative` block added only where a Dflash draft exists.

## Critical files (new)
- `scripts/build_trtllm_engines.sh` (stub)

toolUseResult.content:
# Enable Dflash Speculative Decoding (vLLM + SGLang + TensorRT-LLM)

## Context

Phase 1 benchmarking and Phase 3 quantization benchmarks measure throughput / latency / quality across three serving backends. Speculative decoding (SD) is a well-established way to cut decode latency 2–6× without changing model quality, but the project's three launchers (`serving/launch_vllm.sh`, `launch_sglang.sh`, `launch_trtllm.sh`) emit no SD flags today. The user wants **Dflash** (block-diffusion-based SD; arXiv 2602.06036; up to 2.5× over EAGLE-3), configurable per-engine via YAML and overridable from the benchmark runners so we can A/B "base vs +dflash" cleanly inside the existing `run_exp_*.sh` / `run_concurrency_benchmark.sh` workflow.

Dflash needs a **separate Dflash-trained draft model** paired with the target. The only public pair right now is `meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct` + `z-lab/LLaMA3.1-8B-Instruct-DFlash-UltraChat`; drafts for the project's Qwen3/Gemma/Mistral targets do not yet exist on HF Hub. Training drafts is **out of scope** for this change — this plan ships the *wiring* so that when a Dflash draft is available (HF Hub, NVIDIA ModelOpt PR #1211, or trained in-house) any of the three engines can be launched with one YAML edit or one CLI flag.

Engine support matrix as of 2026-05:
| Engine | Mode | CLI shape | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| **vLLM** | runtime | `--speculative-config '{"method":"dflash","model":"...","num_speculative_tokens":N,...}'` | "Under active development" via `speculators` subproject |
| **SGLang** | runtime | `--speculative-algorithm DFLASH --speculative-draft-model-path <path> [--speculative-num-draft-tokens N] [--speculative-dflash-block-size N] [--speculative-dflash-draft-window-size W]` | Documented; hard constraints (no dp-attn, `pp_size==1`) |
| **TRT-LLM** | **build-time** | Engine pre-built via `modelopt_recipes/general/speculative_decoding/dflash.yaml`; runtime passthrough `--speculative_decoding_mode dflash` if `trtllm-serve` exposes it | NVIDIA ModelOpt PR #1211, not yet merged |

## Approach

Extend the existing per-engine sibling YAMLs (`<model>.yaml`, `<model>_sglang.yaml`, `<model>_trtllm.yaml`) with a `serving.speculative:` sub-block using **engine-native field names** (matching the existing convention that vLLM/SGLang/TRT-LLM YAML keys are not unified). Each launcher reads only the fields it understands and emits the right server CLI args. Add four unified CLI overrides to the launchers and benchmark scripts (`--speculative-method`, `--speculative-draft-model`, `--speculative-num-tokens`, `--no-speculative`) so A/B runs need no YAML edits. Pydantic schema gets a permissive `SpeculativeConfig` sub-model (informational only — bash launchers bypass it). TRT-LLM engine builder is deferred behind a stub script (Option B: require `source: engine_dir` for Dflash; error clearly otherwise).

## YAML schema — engine-native, opt-in via `enabled: true`

### vLLM (e.g. `configs/models_exp_a/qwen3_32b.yaml`)
```yaml
serving:
  engine: "vllm"
  # ... existing fields ...
  speculative:
    enabled: true
    method: "dflash"                                # vLLM speculators method name
    draft_model: "z-lab/LLaMA3.1-8B-Instruct-DFlash-UltraChat"
    num_speculative_tokens: 5
    dflash_block_size: 4                            # optional; merged into JSON
    dflash_draft_window_size: 16                    # optional; merged into JSON
    extra: {}                                       # escape hatch — forward-compat
```
Emits: `--speculative-config '{"method":"dflash","model":"...","num_speculative_tokens":5,"dflash_block_size":4,"dflash_draft_window_size":16}'`

### SGLang (e.g. `configs/models_exp_a/qwen3_32b_sglang.yaml`)
```yaml
serving:
  engine: "sglang"
  # ... existing fields ...
  speculative:
    enabled: true
    algorithm: "DFLASH"
    draft_model_path: "z-lab/LLaMA3.1-8B-Instruct-DFlash-UltraChat"
    num_draft_tokens: 5
    dflash_block_size: 4
    dflash_draft_window_size: 16
```
Emits the five `--speculative-*` flags listed in the matrix.

### TRT-LLM (e.g. `configs/models_exp_a/qwen3_32b_trtllm.yaml`)
```yaml
serving:
  engine: "tensorrt_llm"
  source: "engine_dir"                              # MANDATORY when speculative.enabled
  engine_dir: "checkpoints/trt-engines/qwen3_32b_bf16_dflash"
  # ... existing fields ...
  speculative:
    enabled: true
    mode: "dflash"                                  # → --speculative_decoding_mode dflash
    build_recipe: "modelopt_recipes/general/speculative_decoding/dflash.yaml"
    build_params:                                   # echoed in banner; not forwarded
      block_size: 4
      num_layers: 1
      mask_token_id: 0
      num_anchors: 16
      attention_heads: 8
```

**Design call — keep `num_speculative_tokens` and `dflash_block_size` as two fields.** They control different things: `num_speculative_tokens` is the per-step proposal count (tunable at serve time); `dflash_block_size` is a draft-architecture parameter that must match the trained draft. Collapsing them loses A/B flexibility.

## Files to modify

### `serving/launch_vllm.sh` (vLLM launcher)
Parse the new keys after L74 with the existing inline-python helper (extend to handle dict→JSON for `extra`). Build the speculative JSON in a single python block parallel to the `HF_OVERRIDES` block at L80-87. Append `--speculative-config "$SPEC_JSON"` to `VLLM_ARGS` after L150.

Extend the CLI argparse while-loop (L100-111) with:
- `--speculative-method <name>`
- `--speculative-draft-model <path>`
- `--speculative-num-tokens <int>`
- `--no-speculative` (force-disable; CLI > YAML, mirroring `--max-model-len` precedence at L113-116)

Add a `WARN` (do not block) when `KV_CACHE_DTYPE` matches `turboquant*` or `rotorquant*` AND `speculative.enabled=true`: the case-arm at L170-177 forwards argv unchanged, so `--speculative-config` reaches the in-process launcher untouched — but the engine-config hook in `launch_vllm_turboquant.py` patches `is_hybrid`/`is_mm_prefix_lm` during `create_engine_config`, which is where the draft model also loads. The combo is **untested**; print a one-line warning naming the interaction.

### `serving/launch_sglang.sh` (SGLang launcher)
Parse the new keys after L74. Append the five `--speculative-*` flags to `SGLANG_ARGS` after L143. Extend the argparse while-loop (L83-94) with the same four unified overrides; translate them to SGLang's native flag names so the parent runner uses one vocabulary across engines.

**Hard guards (exit 1):** Reject `--enable-dp-attention=true` AND `speculative.enabled=true` ("DFLASH is incompatible with `--enable-dp-attention`"). Reject `pp_size > 1` (currently not parsed — add it with default 1). Warn that SGLang internally disables overlap scheduler and mixed-chunked-prefill when DFLASH is on; no flag needed from us.

### `serving/launch_trtllm.sh` (TRT-LLM launcher)
Parse `serving.speculative.enabled` and `serving.speculative.mode` after L65. Append `--speculative_decoding_mode "$SPEC_MODE"` to `TRT_ARGS` after L112 — **verify against `trtllm-serve --help`** at implementation time; if the flag is absent in our installed version, the engine config already encodes the mode and no flag is needed (no-op).

Extend the argparse while-loop (L69-80) with the same four unified overrides — but log `--speculative-draft-model` / `--speculative-num-tokens` as **ignored** with a note that these are build-time for TRT-LLM.

**Hard guards (exit 1):** Reject `speculative.enabled=true` with `source: hf` ("Dflash requires a pre-built TRT-LLM engine; see scripts/build_trtllm_engines.sh"). Reject `speculative.enabled=true` with missing `engine_dir`.

### `src/llm_workflow_agents/serving/launch_vllm_turboquant.py` and `launch_vllm_rotorquant.py`
**No argparse changes required.** Both launchers forward argv to `api_server.make_arg_parser()`, which already recognises stock vLLM's `--speculative-config`. The bash launcher resolves the unified `--speculative-method` / `--speculative-draft-model` / `--speculative-num-tokens` flags into a single `--speculative-config '<json>'` *before* invoking the python module, so these files see only stock vLLM flags.

Add a one-line docstring note to `_install_turboquant_engine_config_hook` documenting the untested interaction with speculative drafts that share Gemma-4 architecture (the `is_mm_prefix_lm` patch would propagate to the draft loader). Current Dflash drafts are Llama-3.1, so no interaction in practice.

### `src/llm_workflow_agents/config/schema.py` (Pydantic schema, L44-52)
Add a permissive `SpeculativeConfig` model with `model_config = {"extra": "allow"}` and **all fields optional**. Cover the union of vLLM/SGLang/TRT-LLM keys (`enabled`, `method`, `draft_model`, `num_speculative_tokens`, `algorithm`, `draft_model_path`, `num_draft_tokens`, `dflash_block_size`, `dflash_draft_window_size`, `mode`, `build_recipe`, `build_params`, `extra`). Add `speculative: SpeculativeConfig | None = None` to `ServingConfig`. Schema stays informational — bash launchers bypass Pydantic today.

### `scripts/run_exp_a.sh`
Extend the while-loop at L31-43 with the four unified CLI flags; forward them verbatim to the `bash "$LAUNCH_SCRIPT" "$MODEL_CONFIG" ...` invocation at L137. Add a "Speculative:" line to the banner (L84-90). **Include `_spec-<method>` (or `_spec-off`) in the result filename at L162** so A/B runs don't overwrite each other.

### `scripts/run_concurrency_benchmark.sh`
Same four CLI flags, appended to the launcher invocation at L267-270. Add `_spec-*` suffix to result filename (L219). The existing TRT-LLM 30-min `HEALTH_ITERS=360` budget (L285) covers the slower engine-load path when speculative is enabled — no change needed there.

### `scripts/build_trtllm_engines.sh` (NEW — stub)
Accept `<model_config_trtllm.yaml> --output-dir <path> [--recipe dflash]`. Echo the exact `modelopt` command that *would* run, derived from the YAML's `speculative.build_recipe` and `build_params`. Exit 1 with: "Engine builder not yet wired; NVIDIA ModelOpt PR #1211 required. Hand-build with the printed command and place the result at `serving.engine_dir`." This makes the missing-builder failure surface usefully instead of as a generic "engine_dir not found".

### `tests/test_serving_launch_dispatch.py`
Enable a `LAUNCH_PRINT_ONLY=1` env var in each launcher that replaces the final `exec` with `echo "$@"; exit 0` (one-line diff per launcher). Then add:

1. `test_vllm_launcher_emits_speculative_config_json_when_enabled` — assert printed argv contains `--speculative-config`; parse the JSON and assert the expected dict.
2. `test_vllm_launcher_omits_speculative_when_disabled` — block absent or `enabled=false` → no `--speculative-config`.
3. `test_vllm_cli_override_no_speculative_trumps_yaml` — CLI `--no-speculative` wins over YAML `enabled: true`.
4. `test_sglang_launcher_emits_dflash_flags` — verify all five `--speculative-*` flags in argv.
5. `test_sglang_rejects_dp_attention_with_speculative` — exit 1, stderr contains `dp-attention`.
6. `test_sglang_rejects_pp_size_gt_1_with_speculative` — exit 1, stderr mentions `pp_size`.
7. `test_trtllm_rejects_hf_source_with_speculative` — exit 1, stderr mentions `engine_dir`.
8. `test_trtllm_emits_speculative_mode_when_engine_dir` — `source: engine_dir` + mocked-existing dir → argv contains `--speculative_decoding_mode dflash`.
9. `test_speculative_config_pydantic_roundtrip` — load each engine YAML through the Pydantic schema and assert `serving.speculative.enabled is True`.
10. `test_run_exp_a_forwards_speculative_cli` — invoke `run_exp_a.sh --dry-run ...` and grep the dry-run output for the forwarded flags.

## Verification (end-to-end)

Until a Qwen/Gemma/Mistral Dflash draft exists, smoke-test with the public Llama-3.1 pair via temporary triplet `configs/models_exp_a/llama31_8b_dflash{,_sglang,_trtllm}.yaml`.

```bash
# vLLM
bash serving/launch.sh configs/models_exp_a/llama31_8b_dflash.yaml --port 8000
curl -s http://localhost:8000/v1/chat/completions -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"model":"meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct","messages":[{"role":"user","content":"Hello"}],"max_tokens":64}' | jq .
# Confirm `--speculative-config` in vLLM startup log; check
# `vllm:spec_decode_num_accepted_tokens_total` Prometheus counter is non-zero.

# SGLang
bash serving/launch.sh configs/models_exp_a/llama31_8b_dflash_sglang.yaml --port 8000
# Verify SGLang banner shows speculative-algorithm=DFLASH; /get_server_info reports a draft worker.

# TRT-LLM (requires pre-built engine; otherwise verify the guard fires)
bash serving/launch.sh configs/models_exp_a/llama31_8b_dflash_trtllm.yaml --port 8000

# A/B differential via the runner
bash scripts/run_concurrency_benchmark.sh --config configs/models_exp_a/llama31_8b.yaml \
     --speculative-method dflash \
     --speculative-draft-model z-lab/LLaMA3.1-8B-Instruct-DFlash-UltraChat \
     --speculative-num-tokens 5
bash scripts/run_concurrency_benchmark.sh --config configs/models_exp_a/llama31_8b.yaml --no-speculative
# Compare *_spec-dflash vs *_spec-off result JSONs: TPOT should drop; acceptance rate should be reported.

# Negative path
bash serving/launch.sh configs/models_exp_a/qwen3_32b_trtllm.yaml  # source=hf + speculative → exit 1
# Manually set serving.enable_dp_attention=true in an SGLang config → exit 1 with "dp-attention" in stderr.

# Test suite
.venv/bin/pytest tests/test_serving_launch_dispatch.py -v
```

## Out of scope (explicitly deferred)

- Training Dflash draft models for Qwen3 / Gemma / Mistral targets (depends on ModelOpt PR #1211 or SafeAILab tooling).
- TRT-LLM engine build automation (`scripts/build_trtllm_engines.sh` is a stub; real builder lands once PR #1211 is merged and we have a draft to build with).
- Updating `eval/agent_benchmark.py` payload shapes — Dflash is transparent to the OpenAI-compatible API; no client-side change needed.
- Per-engine spec-decode metric scraping (acceptance rate, draft TPOT) — could go in `eval/concurrency_benchmark.py` as a follow-up; this plan only ensures the engines run with SD enabled.

## Critical files (modified)
- `serving/launch_vllm.sh`
- `serving/launch_sglang.sh`
- `serving/launch_trtllm.sh`
- `src/llm_workflow_agents/serving/launch_vllm_turboquant.py` (docstring only)
- `src/llm_workflow_agents/config/schema.py`
- `scripts/run_exp_a.sh`
- `scripts/run_concurrency_benchmark.sh`
- `tests/test_serving_launch_dispatch.py`
- Per-model YAMLs in `configs/models_exp_a/` and `configs/models_exp_bc/` — opt-in `serving.speculative` block added only where a Dflash draft exists.

## Critical files (new)
- `scripts/build_trtllm_engines.sh` (stub)
- `configs/models_exp_a/llama31_8b_dflash.yaml` and `_sglash.yaml` / `_trtllm.yaml` siblings (smoke-test triplet)

# Enable Dflash Speculative Decoding (vLLM + SGLang + TensorRT-LLM)

## Context

Phase 1 benchmarking and Phase 3 quantization benchmarks measure throughput / latency / quality across three serving backends. Speculative decoding (SD) is a well-established way to cut decode latency 2–6× without changing model quality, but the project's three launchers (`serving/launch_vllm.sh`, `launch_sglang.sh`, `launch_trtllm.sh`) emit no SD flags today. The user wants **Dflash** (block-diffusion-based SD; arXiv 2602.06036; up to 2.5× over EAGLE-3), configurable per-engine via YAML and overridable from the benchmark runners so we can A/B "base vs +dflash" cleanly inside the existing `run_exp_*.sh` / `run_concurrency_benchmark.sh` workflow.

Dflash needs a **separate Dflash-trained draft model** paired with the target. The only public pair right now is `meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct` + `z-lab/LLaMA3.1-8B-Instruct-DFlash-UltraChat`; drafts for the project's Qwen3/Gemma/Mistral targets do not yet exist on HF Hub. Training drafts is **out of scope** for this change — this plan ships the *wiring* so that when a Dflash draft is available (HF Hub, NVIDIA ModelOpt PR #1211, or trained in-house) any of the three engines can be launched with one YAML edit or one CLI flag.

Engine support matrix as of 2026-05:
| Engine | Mode | CLI shape | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| **vLLM** | runtime | `--speculative-config '{"method":"dflash","model":"...","num_speculative_tokens":N,...}'` | "Under active development" via `speculators` subproject |
| **SGLang** | runtime | `--speculative-algorithm DFLASH --speculative-draft-model-path <path> [--speculative-num-draft-tokens N] [--speculative-dflash-block-size N] [--speculative-dflash-draft-window-size W]` | Documented; hard constraints (no dp-attn, `pp_size==1`) |
| **TRT-LLM** | **build-time** | Engine pre-built via `modelopt_recipes/general/speculative_decoding/dflash.yaml`; runtime passthrough `--speculative_decoding_mode dflash` if `trtllm-serve` exposes it | NVIDIA ModelOpt PR #1211, not yet merged |

## Approach

Extend the existing per-engine sibling YAMLs (`<model>.yaml`, `<model>_sglang.yaml`, `<model>_trtllm.yaml`) with a `serving.speculative:` sub-block using **engine-native field names** (matching the existing convention that vLLM/SGLang/TRT-LLM YAML keys are not unified).
```yaml
serving:
  engine: "vllm"
  # ... existing fields ...
  speculative:
    enabled: true
    method: "dflash"                                # vLLM speculators method name
    draft_model: "z-lab/LLaMA3.1-8B-Instruct-DFlash-UltraChat"
    num_speculative_tokens: 5
    dflash_block_size: 4                            # optional; merged into JSON
    dflash_draft_window_size: 16                    # optional; merged into JSON
    extra: {}                                       # escape hatch — forward-compat
```
Emits: `--speculative-config '{"method":"dflash","model":"...","num_speculative_tokens":5,"dflash_block_size":4,"dflash_draft_window_size":16}'`

### SGLang (e.g. `configs/models_exp_a/qwen3_32b_sglang.yaml`)
```yaml
serving:
  engine: "sglang"
  # ... existing fields ...
  speculative:
    enabled: true
    algorithm: "DFLASH"
    draft_model_path: "z-lab/LLaMA3.1-8B-Instruct-DFlash-UltraChat"
    num_draft_tokens: 5
    dflash_block_size: 4
    dflash_draft_window_size: 16
```
Emits the five `--speculative-*` flags listed in the matrix.

### TRT-LLM (e.g. `configs/models_exp_a/qwen3_32b_trtllm.yaml`)
```yaml
serving:
  engine: "tensorrt_llm"
  source: "engine_dir"                              # MANDATORY when speculative.enabled
  engine_dir: "checkpoints/trt-engines/qwen3_32b_bf16_dflash"
  # ... existing fields ...
  speculative:
    enabled: true
    mode: "dflash"                                  # → --speculative_decoding_mode dflash
    build_recipe: "modelopt_recipes/general/speculative_decoding/dflash.yaml"
    build_params:                                   # echoed in banner; not forwarded
      block_size: 4
      num_layers: 1
      mask_token_id: 0
      num_anchors: 16
      attention_heads: 8
```

**Design call — keep `num_speculative_tokens` and `dflash_block_size` as two fields.** They control different things: `num_speculative_tokens` is the per-step proposal count (tunable at serve time); `dflash_block_size` is a draft-architecture parameter that must match the trained draft. ## Approved Plan:
# Enable Dflash Speculative Decoding (vLLM + SGLang + TensorRT-LLM)

## Context

Phase 1 benchmarking and Phase 3 quantization benchmarks measure throughput / latency / quality across three serving backends. Speculative decoding (SD) is a well-established way to cut decode latency 2–6× without changing model quality, but the project's three launchers (`serving/launch_vllm.sh`, `launch_sglang.sh`, `launch_trtllm.sh`) emit no SD flags today. The user wants **Dflash** (block-diffusion-based SD; arXiv 2602.06036; up to 2.5× over EAGLE-3), configurable per-engine via YAML and overridable from the benchmark runners so we can A/B "base vs +dflash" cleanly inside the existing `run_exp_*.sh` / `run_concurrency_benchmark.sh` workflow.

Dflash needs a **separate Dflash-trained draft model** paired with the target. The only public pair right now is `meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct` + `z-lab/LLaMA3.1-8B-Instruct-DFlash-UltraChat`; drafts for the project's Qwen3/Gemma/Mistral targets do not yet exist on HF Hub. Training drafts is **out of scope** for this change — this plan ships the *wiring* so that when a Dflash draft is available (HF Hub, NVIDIA ModelOpt PR #1211, or trained in-house) any of the three engines can be launched with one YAML edit or one CLI flag.

Engine support matrix as of 2026-05:
| Engine | Mode | CLI shape | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| **vLLM** | runtime | `--speculative-config '{"method":"dflash","model":"...","num_speculative_tokens":N,...}'` | "Under active development" via `speculators` subproject |
| **SGLang** | runtime | `--speculative-algorithm DFLASH --speculative-draft-model-path <path> [--speculative-num-draft-tokens N] [--speculative-dflash-block-size N] [--speculative-dflash-draft-window-size W]` | Documented; hard constraints (no dp-attn, `pp_size==1`) |
| **TRT-LLM** | **build-time** | Engine pre-built via `modelopt_recipes/general/speculative_decoding/dflash.yaml`; runtime passthrough `--speculative_decoding_mode dflash` if `trtllm-serve` exposes it | NVIDIA ModelOpt PR #1211, not yet merged |

## Approach

Extend the existing per-engine sibling YAMLs (`<model>.yaml`, `<model>_sglang.yaml`, `<model>_trtllm.yaml`) with a `serving.speculative:` sub-block using **engine-native field names** (matching the existing convention that vLLM/SGLang/TRT-LLM YAML keys are not unified). Each launcher reads only the fields it understands and emits the right server CLI args. Add four unified CLI overrides to the launchers and benchmark scripts (`--speculative-method`, `--speculative-draft-model`, `--speculative-num-tokens`, `--no-speculative`) so A/B runs need no YAML edits. Pydantic schema gets a permissive `SpeculativeConfig` sub-model (informational only — bash launchers bypass it). TRT-LLM engine builder is deferred behind a stub script (Option B: require `source: engine_dir` for Dflash; error clearly otherwise).

## YAML schema — engine-native, opt-in via `enabled: true`

### vLLM (e.g. `configs/models_exp_a/qwen3_32b.yaml`)
```yaml
serving:
  engine: "vllm"
  # ... existing fields ...
  speculative:
    enabled: true
    method: "dflash"                                # vLLM speculators method name
    draft_model: "z-lab/LLaMA3.1-8B-Instruct-DFlash-UltraChat"
    num_speculative_tokens: 5
    dflash_block_size: 4                            # optional; merged into JSON
    dflash_draft_window_size: 16                    # optional; merged into JSON
    extra: {}                                       # escape hatch — forward-compat
```
Emits: `--speculative-config '{"method":"dflash","model":"...","num_speculative_tokens":5,"dflash_block_size":4,"dflash_draft_window_size":16}'`

### SGLang (e.g. `configs/models_exp_a/qwen3_32b_sglang.yaml`)
```yaml
serving:
  engine: "sglang"
  # ... existing fields ...
  speculative:
    enabled: true
    algorithm: "DFLASH"
    draft_model_path: "z-lab/LLaMA3.1-8B-Instruct-DFlash-UltraChat"
    num_draft_tokens: 5
    dflash_block_size: 4
    dflash_draft_window_size: 16
```
Emits the five `--speculative-*` flags listed in the matrix.

### TRT-LLM (e.g. `configs/models_exp_a/qwen3_32b_trtllm.yaml`)
```yaml
serving:
  engine: "tensorrt_llm"
  source: "engine_dir"                              # MANDATORY when speculative.enabled
  engine_dir: "checkpoints/trt-engines/qwen3_32b_bf16_dflash"
  # ... existing fields ...
  speculative:
    enabled: true
    mode: "dflash"                                  # → --speculative_decoding_mode dflash
    build_recipe: "modelopt_recipes/general/speculative_decoding/dflash.yaml"
    build_params:                                   # echoed in banner; not forwarded
      block_size: 4
      num_layers: 1
      mask_token_id: 0
      num_anchors: 16
      attention_heads: 8
```

**Design call — keep `num_speculative_tokens` and `dflash_block_size` as two fields.** They control different things: `num_speculative_tokens` is the per-step proposal count (tunable at serve time); `dflash_block_size` is a draft-architecture parameter that must match the trained draft. Collapsing them loses A/B flexibility.

## Files to modify

### `serving/launch_vllm.sh` (vLLM launcher)
Parse the new keys after L74 with the existing inline-python helper (extend to handle dict→JSON for `extra`). Build the speculative JSON in a single python block parallel to the `HF_OVERRIDES` block at L80-87. Append `--speculative-config "$SPEC_JSON"` to `VLLM_ARGS` after L150.

Extend the CLI argparse while-loop (L100-111) with:
- `--speculative-method <name>`
- `--speculative-draft-model <path>`
- `--speculative-num-tokens <int>`
- `--no-speculative` (force-disable; CLI > YAML, mirroring `--max-model-len` precedence at L113-116)

Add a `WARN` (do not block) when `KV_CACHE_DTYPE` matches `turboquant*` or `rotorquant*` AND `speculative.enabled=true`: the case-arm at L170-177 forwards argv unchanged, so `--speculative-config` reaches the in-process launcher untouched — but the engine-config hook in `launch_vllm_turboquant.py` patches `is_hybrid`/`is_mm_prefix_lm` during `create_engine_config`, which is where the draft model also loads. The combo is **untested**; print a one-line warning naming the interaction.

### `serving/launch_sglang.sh` (SGLang launcher)
Parse the new keys after L74. Append the five `--speculative-*` flags to `SGLANG_ARGS` after L143. Extend the argparse while-loop (L83-94) with the same four unified overrides; translate them to SGLang's native flag names so the parent runner uses one vocabulary across engines.

**Hard guards (exit 1):** Reject `--enable-dp-attention=true` AND `speculative.enabled=true` ("DFLASH is incompatible with `--enable-dp-attention`"). Reject `pp_size > 1` (currently not parsed — add it with default 1). Warn that SGLang internally disables overlap scheduler and mixed-chunked-prefill when DFLASH is on; no flag needed from us.

### `serving/launch_trtllm.sh` (TRT-LLM launcher)
Parse `serving.speculative.enabled` and `serving.speculative.mode` after L65. Append `--speculative_decoding_mode "$SPEC_MODE"` to `TRT_ARGS` after L112 — **verify against `trtllm-serve --help`** at implementation time; if the flag is absent in our installed version, the engine config already encodes the mode and no flag is needed (no-op).

Extend the argparse while-loop (L69-80) with the same four unified overrides — but log `--speculative-draft-model` / `--speculative-num-tokens` as **ignored** with a note that these are build-time for TRT-LLM.

**Hard guards (exit 1):** Reject `speculative.enabled=true` with `source: hf` ("Dflash requires a pre-built TRT-LLM engine; see scripts/build_trtllm_engines.sh"). Reject `speculative.enabled=true` with missing `engine_dir`.

### `src/llm_workflow_agents/serving/launch_vllm_turboquant.py` and `launch_vllm_rotorquant.py`
**No argparse changes required.** Both launchers forward argv to `api_server.make_arg_parser()`, which already recognises stock vLLM's `--speculative-config`. The bash launcher resolves the unified `--speculative-method` / `--speculative-draft-model` / `--speculative-num-tokens` flags into a single `--speculative-config '<json>'` *before* invoking the python module, so these files see only stock vLLM flags.

Add a one-line docstring note to `_install_turboquant_engine_config_hook` documenting the untested interaction with speculative drafts that share Gemma-4 architecture (the `is_mm_prefix_lm` patch would propagate to the draft loader). Current Dflash drafts are Llama-3.1, so no interaction in practice.

### `src/llm_workflow_agents/config/schema.py` (Pydantic schema, L44-52)
Add a permissive `SpeculativeConfig` model with `model_config = {"extra": "allow"}` and **all fields optional**. Cover the union of vLLM/SGLang/TRT-LLM keys (`enabled`, `method`, `draft_model`, `num_speculative_tokens`, `algorithm`, `draft_model_path`, `num_draft_tokens`, `dflash_block_size`, `dflash_draft_window_size`, `mode`, `build_recipe`, `build_params`, `extra`). Add `speculative: SpeculativeConfig | None = None` to `ServingConfig`. Schema stays informational — bash launchers bypass Pydantic today.

### `scripts/run_exp_a.sh`
Extend the while-loop at L31-43 with the four unified CLI flags; forward them verbatim to the `bash "$LAUNCH_SCRIPT" "$MODEL_CONFIG" ...` invocation at L137. Add a "Speculative:" line to the banner (L84-90). **Include `_spec-<method>` (or `_spec-off`) in the result filename at L162** so A/B runs don't overwrite each other.

### `scripts/run_concurrency_benchmark.sh`
Same four CLI flags, appended to the launcher invocation at L267-270. Add `_spec-*` suffix to result filename (L219). The existing TRT-LLM 30-min `HEALTH_ITERS=360` budget (L285) covers the slower engine-load path when speculative is enabled — no change needed there.

### `scripts/build_trtllm_engines.sh` (NEW — stub)
Accept `<model_config_trtllm.yaml> --output-dir <path> [--recipe dflash]`. Echo the exact `modelopt` command that *would* run, derived from the YAML's `speculative.build_recipe` and `build_params`. Exit 1 with: "Engine builder not yet wired; NVIDIA ModelOpt PR #1211 required. Hand-build with the printed command and place the result at `serving.engine_dir`." This makes the missing-builder failure surface usefully instead of as a generic "engine_dir not found".

### `tests/test_serving_launch_dispatch.py`
Enable a `LAUNCH_PRINT_ONLY=1` env var in each launcher that replaces the final `exec` with `echo "$@"; exit 0` (one-line diff per launcher). Then add:

1. `test_vllm_launcher_emits_speculative_config_json_when_enabled` — assert printed argv contains `--speculative-config`; parse the JSON and assert the expected dict.

8. `test_trtllm_emits_speculative_mode_when_engine_dir` — `source: engine_dir` + mocked-existing dir → argv contains `--speculative_decoding_mode dflash`.

```yaml
    extra: {}                                       # escape hatch — forward-compat
```
Emits: `--speculative-config '{"method":"dflash","model":"...","num_speculative_tokens":5,"dflash_block_size":4,"dflash_draft_window_size":16}'`

### SGLang (e.g. `configs/models_exp_a/qwen3_32b_sglang.yaml`)
```yaml
serving:
  engine: "sglang"
  # ... existing fields ...
  speculative:
    enabled: true
    algorithm: "DFLASH"
    draft_model_path: "z-lab/LLaMA3.1-8B-Instruct-DFlash-UltraChat"
    num_draft_tokens: 5
    dflash_block_size: 4
    dflash_draft_window_size: 16
```
Emits the five `--speculative-*` flags listed in the matrix.

### TRT-LLM (e.g. `configs/models_exp_a/qwen3_32b_trtllm.yaml`)
```yaml
serving:
  engine: "tensorrt_llm"
  source: "engine_dir"                              # MANDATORY when speculative.enabled
  engine_dir: "checkpoints/trt-engines/qwen3_32b_bf16_dflash"
  # ... existing fields ...
  speculative:
    enabled: true
    mode: "dflash"                                  # → --speculative_decoding_mode dflash
    build_recipe: "modelopt_recipes/general/speculative_decoding/dflash.yaml"
    build_params:                                   # echoed in banner; not forwarded
      block_size: 4
      num_layers: 1
      mask_token_id: 0
      num_anchors: 16
      attention_heads: 8
```

**Design call — keep `num_speculative_tokens` and `dflash_block_size` as two fields.** They control different things: `num_speculative_tokens` is the per-step proposal count (tunable at serve time); `dflash_block_size` is a draft-architecture parameter that must match the trained draft. Collapsing them loses A/B flexibility.

## Files to modify

### `serving/launch_vllm.sh` (vLLM launcher)
Parse the new keys after L74 with the existing inline-python helper (extend to handle dict→JSON for `extra`). Build the speculative JSON in a single python block parallel to the `HF_OVERRIDES` block at L80-87. Append `--speculative-config "$SPEC_JSON"` to `VLLM_ARGS` after L150.

Extend the CLI argparse while-loop (L100-111) with:
- `--speculative-method <name>`
- `--speculative-draft-model <path>`
- `--speculative-num-tokens <int>`
- `--no-speculative` (force-disable; CLI > YAML, mirroring `--max-model-len` precedence at L113-116)

Add a `WARN` (do not block) when `KV_CACHE_DTYPE` matches `turboquant*` or `rotorquant*` AND `speculative.enabled=true`: the case-arm at L170-177 forwards argv unchanged, so `--speculative-config` reaches the in-process launcher untouched — but the engine-config hook in `launch_vllm_turboquant.py` patches `is_hybrid`/`is_mm_prefix_lm` during `create_engine_config`, which is where the draft model also loads. The combo is **untested**; print a one-line warning naming the interaction.

### `serving/launch_sglang.sh` (SGLang launcher)
Parse the new keys after L74. Append the five `--speculative-*` flags to `SGLANG_ARGS` after L143. Extend the argparse while-loop (L83-94) with the same four unified overrides; translate them to SGLang's native flag names so the parent runner uses one vocabulary across engines.

**Hard guards (exit 1):** Reject `--enable-dp-attention=true` AND `speculative.enabled=true` ("DFLASH is incompatible with `--enable-dp-attention`"). Reject `pp_size > 1` (currently not parsed — add it with default 1). Warn that SGLang internally disables overlap scheduler and mixed-chunked-prefill when DFLASH is on; no flag needed from us.

### `serving/launch_trtllm.sh` (TRT-LLM launcher)
Parse `serving.speculative.enabled` and `serving.speculative.mode` after L65. Append `--speculative_decoding_mode "$SPEC_MODE"` to `TRT_ARGS` after L112 — **verify against `trtllm-serve --help`** at implementation time; if the flag is absent in our installed version, the engine config already encodes the mode and no flag is needed (no-op).

Extend the argparse while-loop (L69-80) with the same four unified overrides — but log `--speculative-draft-model` / `--speculative-num-tokens` as **ignored** with a note that these are build-time for TRT-LLM.

**Hard guards (exit 1):** Reject `speculative.enabled=true` with `source: hf` ("Dflash requires a pre-built TRT-LLM engine; see scripts/build_trtllm_engines.sh"). Reject `speculative.enabled=true` with missing `engine_dir`.

### `src/llm_workflow_agents/serving/launch_vllm_turboquant.py` and `launch_vllm_rotorquant.py`
**No argparse changes required.** Both launchers forward argv to `api_server.make_arg_parser()`, which already recognises stock vLLM's `--speculative-config`. The bash launcher resolves the unified `--speculative-method` / `--speculative-draft-model` / `--speculative-num-tokens` flags into a single `--speculative-config '<json>'` *before* invoking the python module, so these files see only stock vLLM flags.

Add a one-line docstring note to `_install_turboquant_engine_config_hook` documenting the untested interaction with speculative drafts that share Gemma-4 architecture (the `is_mm_prefix_lm` patch would propagate to the draft loader). Current Dflash drafts are Llama-3.1, so no interaction in practice.

### `src/llm_workflow_agents/config/schema.py` (Pydantic schema, L44-52)
Add a permissive `SpeculativeConfig` model with `model_config = {"extra": "allow"}` and **all fields optional**. Cover the union of vLLM/SGLang/TRT-LLM keys (`enabled`, `method`, `draft_model`, `num_speculative_tokens`, `algorithm`, `draft_model_path`, `num_draft_tokens`, `dflash_block_size`, `dflash_draft_window_size`, `mode`, `build_recipe`, `build_params`, `extra`). Add `speculative: SpeculativeConfig | None = None` to `ServingConfig`. Schema stays informational — bash launchers bypass Pydantic today.

### `scripts/run_exp_a.sh`
Extend the while-loop at L31-43 with the four unified CLI flags; forward them verbatim to the `bash "$LAUNCH_SCRIPT" "$MODEL_CONFIG" ...` invocation at L137. Add a "Speculative:" line to the banner (L84-90). **Include `_spec-<method>` (or `_spec-off`) in the result filename at L162** so A/B runs don't overwrite each other.

### `scripts/run_concurrency_benchmark.sh`
Same four CLI flags, appended to the launcher invocation at L267-270. Add `_spec-*` suffix to result filename (L219). The existing TRT-LLM 30-min `HEALTH_ITERS=360` budget (L285) covers the slower engine-load path when speculative is enabled — no change needed there.

### `scripts/build_trtllm_engines.sh` (NEW — stub)
Accept `<model_config_trtllm.yaml> --output-dir <path> [--recipe dflash]`. Echo the exact `modelopt` command that *would* run, derived from the YAML's `speculative.build_recipe` and `build_params`. Exit 1 with: "Engine builder not yet wired; NVIDIA ModelOpt PR #1211 required. Hand-build with the printed command and place the result at `serving.engine_dir`." This makes the missing-builder failure surface usefully instead of as a generic "engine_dir not found".

### `tests/test_serving_launch_dispatch.py`
Enable a `LAUNCH_PRINT_ONLY=1` env var in each launcher that replaces the final `exec` with `echo "$@"; exit 0` (one-line diff per launcher). Then add:

1. `test_vllm_launcher_emits_speculative_config_json_when_enabled` — assert printed argv contains `--speculative-config`; parse the JSON and assert the expected dict.
2. `test_vllm_launcher_omits_speculative_when_disabled` — block absent or `enabled=false` → no `--speculative-config`.
3. `test_vllm_cli_override_no_speculative_trumps_yaml` — CLI `--no-speculative` wins over YAML `enabled: true`.
4. `test_sglang_launcher_emits_dflash_flags` — verify all five `--speculative-*` flags in argv.
5. `test_sglang_rejects_dp_attention_with_speculative` — exit 1, stderr contains `dp-attention`.
6. `test_sglang_rejects_pp_size_gt_1_with_speculative` — exit 1, stderr mentions `pp_size`.
7. `test_trtllm_rejects_hf_source_with_speculative` — exit 1, stderr mentions `engine_dir`.
8. `test_trtllm_emits_speculative_mode_when_engine_dir` — `source: engine_dir` + mocked-existing dir → argv contains `--speculative_decoding_mode dflash`.
9. `test_speculative_config_pydantic_roundtrip` — load each engine YAML through the Pydantic schema and assert `serving.speculative.enabled is True`.
10. `test_run_exp_a_forwards_speculative_cli` — invoke `run_exp_a.sh --dry-run ...` and grep the dry-run output for the forwarded flags.

## Verification (end-to-end)

Until a Qwen/Gemma/Mistral Dflash draft exists, smoke-test with the public Llama-3.1 pair via temporary triplet `configs/models_exp_a/llama31_8b_dflash{,_sglang,_trtllm}.yaml`.

```bash
# vLLM
bash serving/launch.sh configs/models_exp_a/llama31_8b_dflash.yaml --port 8000
curl -s http://localhost:8000/v1/chat/completions -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"model":"meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct","messages":[{"role":"user","content":"Hello"}],"max_tokens":64}' | jq .
# Confirm `--speculative-config` in vLLM startup log; check
# `vllm:spec_decode_num_accepted_tokens_total` Prometheus counter is non-zero.

# SGLang
bash serving/launch.sh configs/models_exp_a/llama31_8b_dflash_sglang.yaml --port 8000
# Verify SGLang banner shows speculative-algorithm=DFLASH; /get_server_info reports a draft worker.

# TRT-LLM (requires pre-built engine; otherwise verify the guard fires)
bash serving/launch.sh configs/models_exp_a/llama31_8b_dflash_trtllm.yaml --port 8000

# A/B differential via the runner
bash scripts/run_concurrency_benchmark.sh --config configs/models_exp_a/llama31_8b.yaml \
     --speculative-method dflash \
     --speculative-draft-model z-lab/LLaMA3.1-8B-Instruct-DFlash-UltraChat \
     --speculative-num-tokens 5
bash scripts/run_concurrency_benchmark.sh --config configs/models_exp_a/llama31_8b.yaml --no-speculative
# Compare *_spec-dflash vs *_spec-off result JSONs: TPOT should drop; acceptance rate should be reported.

# Negative path
bash serving/launch.sh configs/models_exp_a/qwen3_32b_trtllm.yaml  # source=hf + speculative → exit 1
# Manually set serving.enable_dp_attention=true in an SGLang config → exit 1 with "dp-attention" in stderr.

# Test suite
.venv/bin/pytest tests/test_serving_launch_dispatch.py -v
```

## Out of scope (explicitly deferred)

- Training Dflash draft models for Qwen3 / Gemma / Mistral targets (depends on ModelOpt PR #1211 or SafeAILab tooling).
- TRT-LLM engine build automation (`scripts/build_trtllm_engines.sh` is a stub; real builder lands once PR #1211 is merged and we have a draft to build with).
- Updating `eval/agent_benchmark.py` payload shapes — Dflash is transparent to the OpenAI-compatible API; no client-side change needed.
- Per-engine spec-decode metric scraping (acceptance rate, draft TPOT) — could go in `eval/concurrency_benchmark.py` as a follow-up; this plan only ensures the engines run with SD enabled.

## Critical files (modified)
- `serving/launch_vllm.sh`
- `serving/launch_sglang.sh`
- `serving/launch_trtllm.sh`
- `src/llm_workflow_agents/serving/launch_vllm_turboquant.py` (docstring only)
- `src/llm_workflow_agents/config/schema.py`
- `scripts/run_exp_a.sh`
- `scripts/run_concurrency_benchmark.sh`
- `tests/test_serving_launch_dispatch.py`
- Per-model YAMLs in `configs/models_exp_a/` and `configs/models_exp_bc/` — opt-in `serving.speculative` block added only where a Dflash draft exists.

## Critical files (new)
- `scripts/build_trtllm_engines.sh` (stub)
- `configs/models_exp_a/llama31_8b_dflash.yaml` and `_sglash.yaml` / `_trtllm.yaml` siblings (smoke-test triplet)

"""Pydantic schema definitions for all configuration types."""

from __future__ import annotations

from enum import StrEnum
from pathlib import Path

from pydantic import BaseModel, Field


# --- Enums ---


class ModelFamily(StrEnum):
    QWEN = "qwen"
    QWEN35 = "qwen35"
    GEMMA = "gemma"
    MISTRAL = "mistral"
    NEMOTRON = "nemotron"
    GLM = "glm"


class KVCacheDtype(StrEnum):
    AUTO = "auto"
    FP8 = "fp8"
    KIVI = "kivi"
    KVQUANT = "kvquant"
    AWQ_FP8 = "awq_fp8"
    TURBOQUANT = "turboquant"
    ROTORQUANT = "rotorquant"


class ComplexityLevel(StrEnum):
    L1 = "L1"
    L2 = "L2"
    L3 = "L3"
    L4 = "L4"
    L5 = "L5"


# --- Config Models ---


class ServingConfig(BaseModel):
    """vLLM serving configuration."""

    engine: str = "vllm"
    tool_call_parser: str = "hermes"
    chat_template: str = "chatml"
    gpu_memory_utilization: float = 0.90
    max_model_len: int = 8192
    enforce_eager: bool = False


class InferenceConfig(BaseModel):
    """Inference parameters."""

    temperature_deterministic: float = 0.0
    temperature_stochastic: float = 0.7
    stochastic_trials: int = 5
    max_tokens: int = 2048


class ModelConfig(BaseModel):
    """Model configuration for Phase 1 benchmarking."""

    name: str
    family: ModelFamily
    architecture: str = "dense_gqa_transformer"
    params_total: int
    params_active: int | None = None
    context_length: int = 131072
    precision: str = "bfloat16"
    vram_estimate_gb: float | None = None

    serving: ServingConfig = Field(default_factory=ServingConfig)
    inference: InferenceConfig = Field(default_factory=InferenceConfig)
    category: str | None = None
    benchmark_tasks: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)


class LoRAConfig(BaseModel):
    """LoRA adapter configuration."""

    rank: int = 64
    alpha: int = 128
    dropout: float = 0.05
    target_modules: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)
    modules_to_save: list[str] | None = None


class TrainingDataConfig(BaseModel):
    """Training data sources and splits."""

    sources: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)
    custom_synthetic_size: int = 15000
    negative_example_ratio: float = 0.15
    splits: dict[str, float] = Field(
        default_factory=lambda: {"train": 0.85, "val": 0.10, "test": 0.05}
    )


class TrainingConfig(BaseModel):
    """Training hyperparameters."""

    learning_rate: float = 2e-4
    lr_scheduler: str = "cosine"
    warmup_ratio: float = 0.03
    effective_batch_size: int = 32
    gradient_accumulation_steps: int = 8
    num_epochs: int = 3
    max_seq_length: int = 4096
    gradient_checkpointing: bool = True
    packing: bool = True
    save_strategy: str = "steps"
    save_steps: int = 500
    eval_steps: int = 500
    metric_for_best_model: str = "eval_loss"

    training_data: TrainingDataConfig = Field(default_factory=TrainingDataConfig)
    hardware_vram_estimate_gb: float | None = None
    mixed_precision: str = "bf16"


class TrainingModelConfig(BaseModel):
    """Model config with LoRA and training settings for Exp B/C."""

    model: ModelConfig
    lora: LoRAConfig = Field(default_factory=LoRAConfig)
    training: TrainingConfig = Field(default_factory=TrainingConfig)


class QuantizationMethodConfig(BaseModel):
    """Quantization method configuration for Experiment D."""

    name: str
    paper: str | None = None
    status: str | None = None
    bit_widths: list[int] = Field(default_factory=lambda: [2, 3, 4])
    head_dimensions: list[int] = Field(default_factory=lambda: [128, 256])
    kv_cache_dtype: str = "auto"
    requires_calibration: bool = False
    benchmark_runs: int = 5
    benchmark_prompts_per_run: int = 500

    quality_tasks: list[str] = Field(
        default_factory=lambda: [
            "wikitext2_ppl",
            "c4_ppl",
            "longbench_15task",
            "needle_in_haystack",
            "tool_call_f1",
        ]
    )
    performance_metrics: list[str] = Field(
        default_factory=lambda: [
            "peak_vram_gb",
            "kv_cache_size_gb",
            "throughput_prefill_tok_s",
            "throughput_decode_tok_s",
            "latency_ttft_ms",
            "latency_tpot_ms",
            "latency_itl_p50_p95_p99",
            "max_concurrent_batch_4096ctx",
        ]
    )


class ComplexitySpec(BaseModel):
    """Workflow complexity level specification."""

    level: ComplexityLevel
    num_states: tuple[int, int]
    branching_factor: tuple[int, int]
    num_tools: int
    chain_depth: int
    nesting_depth: int
    domain: str


class ServingDeploymentConfig(BaseModel):
    """Serving deployment configuration."""

    mode: str = "single_model"
    kv_cache_dtype: str = "auto"
    port: int = 8000
    models: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)


class ExperimentConfig(BaseModel):
    """Top-level experiment configuration."""

    name: str
    description: str = ""
    models: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)
    seed: int = 42
    output_dir: Path = Path("results")


# --- Complexity Level Registry ---


COMPLEXITY_SPECS: dict[ComplexityLevel, ComplexitySpec] = {
    ComplexityLevel.L1: ComplexitySpec(
        level=ComplexityLevel.L1,
        num_states=(3, 4),
        branching_factor=(1, 2),
        num_tools=1,
        chain_depth=0,
        nesting_depth=0,
        domain="faq_lookup",
    ),
    ComplexityLevel.L2: ComplexitySpec(
        level=ComplexityLevel.L2,
        num_states=(5, 7),
        branching_factor=(2, 3),
        num_tools=2,
        chain_depth=1,
        nesting_depth=1,
        domain="order_status_cancel",
    ),
    ComplexityLevel.L3: ComplexitySpec(
        level=ComplexityLevel.L3,
        num_states=(8, 12),
        branching_factor=(3, 5),
        num_tools=4,
        chain_depth=2,
        nesting_depth=2,
        domain="booking_payment",
    ),
    ComplexityLevel.L4: ComplexitySpec(
        level=ComplexityLevel.L4,
        num_states=(13, 20),
        branching_factor=(5, 8),
        num_tools=6,
        chain_depth=3,
        nesting_depth=3,
        domain="it_troubleshoot_escalation",
    ),
    ComplexityLevel.L5: ComplexitySpec(
        level=ComplexityLevel.L5,
        num_states=(21, 30),
        branching_factor=(8, 99),
        num_tools=7,
        chain_depth=4,
        nesting_depth=4,
        domain="multi_dept_workflow",
    ),
}

# --- User Behavior Distribution ---

USER_BEHAVIOR_DISTRIBUTION: dict[str, float] = {
    "cooperative": 0.60,
    "adversarial_probing": 0.15,
    "digressing": 0.10,
    "invalid_tool_inputs": 0.15,
}

TOOL_ERROR_RATE: float = 0.20

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Run Experiment A: Prompt-encoded business logic evaluation.
#
# Launches each 15-30B model via the engine dispatcher (serving/launch.sh),
# runs workflow quality evaluation, and saves results to results/exp_a/.
#
# Usage:
#   ./scripts/run_exp_a.sh [OPTIONS]
#
# Options:
#   --kv-cache-dtype  KV cache quantization dtype (default: auto)
#   --backend <b>     Serving backend: vllm (default), sglang, tensorrt_llm.
#                     When set, uses <model>_<backend>.yaml sibling configs.
#   --data <path>     Benchmark data directory (default: data/output/benchmark/task_a)
#   --max-samples <n> Limit to first N samples per level, 0=all (default: 0)
#   --dry-run         Print commands without executing

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
PROJECT_ROOT="$(cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/.." && pwd)"
RESULTS_DIR="$PROJECT_ROOT/results/exp_a"
LAUNCH_SCRIPT="$PROJECT_ROOT/serving/launch.sh"

KV_CACHE_DTYPE="auto"
BACKEND=""
DATA_DIR="$PROJECT_ROOT/data/output/benchmark/task_a"
MAX_SAMPLES=0
DRY_RUN=false

while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
    case "$1" in
        --kv-cache-dtype) KV_CACHE_DTYPE="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --backend)        BACKEND="$2";         shift 2 ;;
        --data)           DATA_DIR="$2";        shift 2 ;;
        --max-samples)    MAX_SAMPLES="$2";     shift 2 ;;
        --dry-run)        DRY_RUN=true;         shift ;;
        *)
            echo "Unknown argument: $1"
            exit 1
            ;;
    esac
done

mkdir -p "$RESULTS_DIR"

# Base model config list (vLLM default, unchanged)
MODEL_CONFIGS=(
    "configs/models_exp_a/gemma3_27b.yaml"
    "configs/models_exp_a/qwen3_32b.yaml"
    "configs/models_exp_a/qwen35_35b_a3b.yaml"
    "configs/models_exp_a/mistral_24b.yaml"
    "configs/models_exp_a/nemotron_30b.yaml"
    "configs/models_exp_a/glm47_flash.yaml"
    "configs/models_exp_a/gemma4_26b_a4b.yaml"
    "configs/models_exp_a/gemma4_31b.yaml"
    "configs/models_exp_a/qwen36_27b.yaml"
    "configs/models_exp_a/qwen36_27b_fp8.yaml"
)

# When --backend is set, swap each config to its <model>_<backend>.yaml sibling.
if [[ -n "$BACKEND" ]]; then
    case "$BACKEND" in
        vllm|sglang|tensorrt_llm) ;;
        *)
            echo "ERROR: --backend must be one of: vllm, sglang, tensorrt_llm" >&2
            exit 1
            ;;
    esac
    if [[ "$BACKEND" != "vllm" ]]; then
        SWAPPED=()
        for cfg in "${MODEL_CONFIGS[@]}"; do
            sibling="${cfg%.yaml}_${BACKEND}.yaml"
            if [[ -f "$PROJECT_ROOT/$sibling" ]]; then
                SWAPPED+=("$sibling")
            else
                echo "WARN: sibling config not found, skipping: $sibling" >&2
            fi
        done
        MODEL_CONFIGS=("${SWAPPED[@]}")
    fi
fi

echo "=== Experiment A: Prompt-Encoded Business Logic ==="
echo "KV cache dtype: $KV_CACHE_DTYPE"
echo "Backend:        ${BACKEND:-vllm (default)}"
echo "Data dir:       $DATA_DIR"
echo "Results dir:    $RESULTS_DIR"
echo "Max samples:    ${MAX_SAMPLES} (0=all)"
echo "=================================================="

for CONFIG in "${MODEL_CONFIGS[@]}"; do
    MODEL_CONFIG="$PROJECT_ROOT/$CONFIG"
    MODEL_NAME=$(python3 -c "
import yaml, sys
with open(sys.argv[1]) as f:
    c = yaml.safe_load(f)
print(c['model']['name'])
" "$MODEL_CONFIG")

    SERVING_ENGINE=$(python3 -c "
import yaml, sys
with open(sys.argv[1]) as f:
    c = yaml.safe_load(f)
print(c.get('serving', {}).get('engine', 'vllm'))
" "$MODEL_CONFIG")

    SKIP_REASON=$(python3 -c "
import yaml, sys
with open(sys.argv[1]) as f:
    c = yaml.safe_load(f)
print(c.get('serving', {}).get('skip_reason', ''))
" "$MODEL_CONFIG")

    STOCHASTIC_TRIALS=$(python3 -c "
import yaml, sys
with open(sys.argv[1]) as f:
    c = yaml.safe_load(f)
print(c.get('inference', {}).get('stochastic_trials', 5))
" "$MODEL_CONFIG")

    echo ""
    echo "--- Model: $MODEL_NAME (engine: $SERVING_ENGINE) ---"

    if [[ -n "$SKIP_REASON" ]]; then
        echo "SKIP: $SKIP_REASON"
        continue
    fi

    if [ "$DRY_RUN" = true ]; then
        echo "[DRY RUN] Would launch: bash $LAUNCH_SCRIPT $MODEL_CONFIG --kv-cache-dtype $KV_CACHE_DTYPE"
        echo "[DRY RUN] Would run eval for $MODEL_NAME (engine=$SERVING_ENGINE)"
        continue
    fi

    # Launch server in background (dispatch to the correct backend via launch.sh)
    bash "$LAUNCH_SCRIPT" "$MODEL_CONFIG" --kv-cache-dtype "$KV_CACHE_DTYPE" &
    SERVER_PID=$!

    # TRT-LLM JIT-from-HF can take 5-15 min on first launch; allow 30 min.
    if [[ "$SERVING_ENGINE" == "tensorrt_llm" ]]; then
        HEALTH_ITERS=360
    else
        HEALTH_ITERS=60
    fi

    echo "Waiting for $SERVING_ENGINE server (PID $SERVER_PID)..."
    for i in $(seq 1 $HEALTH_ITERS); do
        if curl -sf http://localhost:8000/health > /dev/null 2>&1; then
            echo "Server ready after $((i * 5))s"
            break
        fi
        sleep 5
    done
    if ! curl -sf http://localhost:8000/health > /dev/null 2>&1; then
        echo "ERROR: $SERVING_ENGINE server failed to start for $MODEL_NAME" >&2
        kill -- -"$SERVER_PID" 2>/dev/null || kill "$SERVER_PID" 2>/dev/null || true
        exit 1
    fi

    # Run evaluation
    RESULT_FILE="$RESULTS_DIR/${MODEL_NAME//\//_}_${SERVING_ENGINE}_${KV_CACHE_DTYPE}.json"
    python3 -m llm_workflow_agents.eval.agent_benchmark \
        --model             "$MODEL_NAME" \
        --engine            "$SERVING_ENGINE" \
        --output            "$RESULT_FILE" \
        --data              "$DATA_DIR" \
        --max-samples       "$MAX_SAMPLES" \
        --stochastic-trials "$STOCHASTIC_TRIALS" \
        --log-level         DEBUG \
        2>&1 | tee "${RESULT_FILE%.json}.log" || true

    # Shut down server (process-group kill handles multi-process backends)
    kill -- -"$SERVER_PID" 2>/dev/null || kill "$SERVER_PID" 2>/dev/null || true
    wait "$SERVER_PID" 2>/dev/null || true
    echo "Server stopped."
done

echo ""
echo "=== Experiment A complete. Results in $RESULTS_DIR ==="
